dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: WARNING THIS EPISODE CONTAINS STRONG LANGUAGE THAT MAY NOT BE SUITABLE FOR SOME READERS.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: THAT'LL BE 0.7 DOLLARS. YOU NEED A BAG?

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: GOOD BYE. PLEASE SAY THANK YOU.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I got a night job at a convenience store, yesterday after school.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Hiring PART TIME Nights 6PM-7AM WAGES NEGOTIABLE

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: Are you still hiring for the night shift?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: BINGO! The sign's on the door.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: JACKPOT! Handsome part-timer. Lots of girl customers. Sales skyrocket.

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: I've never worked at a convenience store before.

dialogue.speaker: MANAGER
dialogue.line: Well, can you start right away?

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: Um, how much are you looking to earn?

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: That is... Wow, he's really cool.

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: No, I mean not for me, for a friend of mine.

dialogue.speaker: MANAGER
dialogue.line: Did something bad happen to him?

dialogue.speaker: MANAGER
dialogue.line: Sorry, but this won't work. I don't hire high schoolers.

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: But my friend said I can-

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Come to think of it, students don't have a clue about the names of cigarette brands.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: So many brand names to memorize: Philip Morris, Camel, Marlboro, Kent, Red, Dunhill, Kool, Reall.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I managed to get hired but, why the salary difference?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I have two bodies, so I need expenses for two persons.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: New body for daytime, old body at night.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: School during the day, work during the night.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I was living 12 hours per body to protect each's health.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Why does this happen to me? What if my new body disappears?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: If only this body would go.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The daytime is like a beautiful dream.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: At night I come back to the cold reality.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And the reality is still like a dream; a horrible dream.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Give me four packs of Marlboro Lights.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: What should I do? I can't sell them smokes!

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: Can I see your ID, please??

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Hey, why are we dressed like this? We should look old enough to get smokes.

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: WHERE DID HE GET THIS OLD MAN'S ID?

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: Um, that doesn't look like you.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: NO, SORRY. THIS IS MY ID.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: The jig's up. Let's bounce here.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Hey! LET'S JUST GO SOMEWHERE ELSE!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: GET A BUNCH OF BOOZE TOO, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: YOU KNOW WE'RE THE SAME AGE.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: Ring this up in honor of our truce.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: AND DID I SAY I'M NOT F***ING PAYING?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Why's he in such a bad mood today?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Don't you get it? He's usually like that.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Mira has eyes for the transfer student, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Zack asked her out but she turned him down, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Right, maybe he's pissed because of the new guy.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: Geez, they went on a date, now it's over?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: I HATE ALL OF THEM! THOSE A**HOLES!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: THE NEW D***HEAD SUCKS. HE'S WORKING ON MY MIRA!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: AND THIS TUB OF LARD, SINCE I COULD GET FIRED, SORRY, WAS HITTING ON MIRA!

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: I GOT HIT AGAIN! I'M PISSED!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: HEY! WHY ARE YOU HITTING HIM?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: And I got beat up by Zack again like that.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: He's got a bad temper, what can ya do?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: I hope you can understand. This is insurance. If you report us, we will spread this photo. Got it?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: HEAD UP, HEAD UP, HANDS BACK.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I was forced to agree on forgetting the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: At least I didn't sell them the smokes. I would've been fired on my first day.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I can't rely on just Mom.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: She's in a good mood, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: SON, WHAT ARE YOU DOING UP THIS LATE?

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: UM, JUST TAKING A WALK, THE NIGHT AIR'S SO FRESH, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: AMAZING! I'M OUT FOR A STROLL TOO! WE'RE ON THE SAME PAGE, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: DANIEL
dialogue.line: SCHOOL? DON'T WORRY! I'M REALLY POPULAR. I WAS JUST WITH A FEW CLASSMATES!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: But only during the day, they beat me up though.

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: YOU KNOW, I'M DOING FINE, BECAUSE YOU'RE AWAY. I'M TAKING THIS NIGHT STROLL.

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: SON, DON'T YOU NEED MORE MONEY?

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: JUST TRUST YOUR MOM AND STUDY HARD. DON'T WORRY ABOUT MONEY. OKAY, SON?

dialogue.speaker: MOM
dialogue.line: hehe. ALL RIGHT, OKAY, I'M GONNA WALK A BIT MORE AND HEAD HOME. I LOVE YOU, SON.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Like that the evening nightmare ended.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I returned to the dreamlike reality.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: So this is what it's like to be buff.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: When you're tall, the world seems tiny.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: Look at his figure! Damn hot.

dialogue.speaker: UNKNOWN
dialogue.line: He must be so happy to come to school with that face.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: They don't know it was me though.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: When I entered the class in this new body, suddenly this prick beat him up!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: I took a picture to ensure he stays silent.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: WANNA SEE OR NOT? hehe. LOL.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK
dialogue.line: YOU THINK I'M PROUD OF HITTING THAT PIG?

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: COME HERE! HE HAS SOMETHING FUNNY TO SHOW US!

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: YESTERDAY ZACK BEAT UP A PIG AND... IF HE SEES IT, HE MIGHT BE SCARED OF ME, hehe.

dialogue.speaker: ZACK'S FRIEND
dialogue.line: IT'S A NUDE PIC OF THE PIGGY, haha.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: They were making fun of me.

summary: Daniel gets a night job at a convenience store to help with his two-body expenses. He encounters his bullies, Zack and his friends, who try to buy cigarettes illegally and then beat him up, threatening him with a humiliating photo. The next day, in his new body at school, Daniel overhears Zack and his friends discussing the previous night's events and the photo they took of his old body.
